pr.title: ft: Match core fighter functions (ftAnim, ftColl, ft_0892, ft_0D31)
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched a batch of core fighter functions across ftAnim, ftColl, ft_0892, and ft_0D31 as part of the PR #2024 split. The visible diff shows several ft_0892 and rebirth-related ft_0D31 functions replacing placeholder comments, plus prototype/type refinements such as ft_80089914, ftColl_80077464, and ftCommonData.x5D8 becoming int. The PR also carried small include/header cleanup and review feedback about an apparently unused .sdata declaration in ftcoll.c.
what_changed: Matched listed animation functions ftAnim_8006C624 and ftAnim_8006C660 according to the PR summary, though their diffs are not present in the excerpted changed-file list.
Matched listed collision functions ftColl_8007AE98, ftColl_8007AED0, ftColl_8007AF0C, ftColl_8007AF4C, and ftColl_8007AF80 according to the PR summary.
Implemented ft_80089768 in ft_0892.c using a local UnkStruct89768 overlay on a Vec2* argument, initializing integer, float, and bitfield members explicitly.
Implemented ft_80089914(HSD_GObj* gobj, int msid), validating attack motion-state IDs and applying character-specific exceptions for Fox, Falco, and Mewtwo.
Implemented ft_8008A1B8(Fighter_GObj* gobj, int flags), storing flags into fp->x221C_u16_y and calling ftParts_8007592C when bit 0x4 is not set.
Implemented ft_8008A1FC(Fighter_GObj* gobj), checking left-stick direction relative to facing_dir and p_ftCommonData->x24 threshold.

Implemented ft_0D31 rebirth helpers fn_800D55B4 and fn_800D5A30. fn_800D55B4 synchronizes a fighter's y position upward to another entity for the same player_id; fn_800D5A30 calls ftColl_8007B7A4 with p_ftCommonData->x5D8 and then ft_8008A2BC.
Updated ft_0892.h prototype for ft_80089914 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 ft_80089914(HSD_GObj* gobj, int msid).
Added prototypes for fn_800D55B4 and fn_800D5A30 in ft_0D31.h.
Updated ftcoll.dox prototype for ftColl_80077464 from unknown parameters to void ftColl_80077464(Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*).
Changed ftCommonData.x5D8 in types.h from UNK_T to int, supporting its use as an integer argument to ftColl_8007B7A4.

Adjusted ftcoll.c include ordering for it/itcoll.h. Review context indicates an added .sdata declaration for ftColl_804D3A68 was questioned as unused, but that declaration is not present in the final excerpt.
smart_moves: Split a larger fighter-core matching effort into a smaller PR focused on core ftAnim, ftColl, ft_0892, and ft_0D31 functions, making review and SHA1 validation more tractable.
Used real typed prototypes in headers/dox instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once call signatures were inferred, improving future callsite matching.
Kept an uncertain data layout local to ft_0892.c as UnkStruct89768 rather than prematurely promoting a speculative type globally.
Documented the MWCC big-endian bitfield ordering directly in the local struct, which is important because bitfield declaration order affects generated code and matching.
Resolved a common-data field type from UNK_T to int only where there was concrete usage evidence: p_ftCommonData->x5D8 is passed as an integer to ftColl_8007B7A4.
Reused existing fighter access conventions such as GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and gobj->user_data consistently rather than introducing new access patterns.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC/PPC big-endian bitfields, declare bitfields in the order that matches the target bit numbering; the diff explicitly notes reversed order for MWCC big-endian and names fields x10_b7 through x10_b0 and x11_b7 through x11_b0.

When a function takes an apparently smaller or misleading pointer type such as Vec2*, a local overlay struct can be used if the assembly clearly writes beyond the nominal fields; keep the type local until the broader structure is understood.
Character-specific behavior can be expressed compactly with motion-state range checks and enum deltas, as in ft_80089914's Fox/Falco/Mewtwo attack-state exclusions.
Header and dox prototypes should be tightened alongside matching work so later functions can call matched routines with real types instead of UNK_PARAMS.
Small common-data field refinements are valuable matching enablers: changing x5D8 to int makes ft_0D31 call patterns clearer and reduces casts/unknowns.
Reviewers may flag declarations introduced during PR splitting/merging that are no longer needed; recheck merge leftovers, especially static data or .sdata declarations.
naming_conventions: Unresolved or partially understood structs in this slice used address-derived names such as UnkStruct89768, preserving uncertainty while making the match searchable.
Bitfield placeholders were named by byte offset and bit index, e.g. x10_b7, x10_b0, x11_b7, x11_b0, matching decomp offset naming style.
Function names retained address-based names where semantic names were not yet known, e.g. ft_80089768, ft_80089914, fn_800D55B4, fn_800D5A30.
Known enum-style constants followed existing fighter naming such as FTKIND_FOX, FTKIND_FALCO, FTKIND_MEWTWO and ftCo_MS_AttackS3Hi / ftCo_MS_AttackS4LwS.
Fighter common-data unknowns kept x-offset naming, e.g. p_ftCommonData->x24 and x5D8, but gained a concrete C type when evidence supported it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit bitfield assignments for individual flag clears when matching code that zeros specific bits rather than a whole byte/word.

For range tests over motion-state IDs, the implementation used unsigned delta comparisons like (u32)(msid - ftCo_MS_AttackS3LwS) <= 2, a common tactic to match compiler output for contiguous enum ranges.
Retain direct gobj->user_data loads where that matches assembly, and use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) where existing inlines produce the desired code shape.
Include only the headers needed for newly matched calls, e.g. adding ftparts.h for ftParts_8007592C, ft_0892.h for ft_8008A2BC, and ftcoll.h for ftColl_8007B7A4.
Avoid over-generalizing uncertain layouts: local typedefs and precise casts can match assembly while limiting blast radius.
review_feedback: r-burns commented on src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c that a newly added .sdata declaration `char* const ftColl_804D3A68;` appeared unused and may have come from a merge with another PR, suggesting it was not needed anymore.
The review feedback implies that PR-split cleanup should include checking for stray data declarations or merge artifacts before merge.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that rename or globalize UnkStruct89768 or its x10/x11 bitfields.
Check whether ftColl_804D3A68 was removed, moved, or matched in another PR after reviewer feedback.
Find subsequent matches that rely on ftCommonData.x5D8 being typed as int.
Look for later semantic naming of fn_800D55B4 and fn_800D5A30 in rebirth/rebirth-wait code.
Compare this PR with PR #2024 to understand what fighter-core work was split out and whether remaining ftAnim/ftColl functions were handled elsewhere.
